articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Law Society probes top lawyer Chipo Mahlangu over church property dispute
more.description: The Law Society of Zimbabwe (LSZ) has confirmed it is probing prominent lawyer Chipo Mahlangu following a complaint by the Assemblies of God-Spiritual Movement (AoG-SM), which alleges gross misconduct in ongoing church property disputes before the courts. The post Law Society probes top lawyer Chipo Mahlangu over church property dispute appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Munhumutapa Cup thrills Central Region clubs
more.description: CENTRAL Region clubs now know who stands in their way after Saturday’s Munhumutapa Cup draw, with the competition set to pull them out of their usual league routine. All 18 teams are in, but the road doesn’t start the same for everyone. Four sides go straight into the first round, while the other 14 wait [...] The post Munhumutapa Cup thrills Central Region clubs appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Zimbabwe Moves to Mainstream Informal Sector as Policy Shift Reframes Economic Role
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s Government has signalled a decisive policy shift towards integrating the informal sector into the mainstream economy, rejecting longstanding perceptions that equate informality with illegality or economic dysfunction. Presidential spokesperson George Charamba said newly approved policy frameworks are designed to reposition the informal economy as a legitimate and productive component of national development. [...] The post Zimbabwe Moves to Mainstream Informal Sector as Policy Shift Reframes Economic Role appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Manufacturing Rebound Fuels Job Creation as Zimbabwe Targets Inclusive Growth
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s manufacturing sector is showing renewed momentum, with rising industrial output and strong small-to-medium enterprise (SME) activity driving job creation and underpinning broader economic recovery, government officials have said. The positive outlook comes ahead of International Workers’ Day commemorations, as authorities highlight employment gains linked to improved industrial performance. more.title: Dangote’s Net Worth Jumps $3.21bn as Refinery Reshapes Nigeria’s Energy Economy
more.description: LAGOS — Africa’s richest businessman, Aliko Dangote, has recorded a sharp increase in personal wealth in 2026, with his net worth rising by $3.21 billion amid sweeping changes in Nigeria’s petroleum sector. more.title: Nissan Redirects $45m Investment to Egypt, Scaling Back South African Manufacturing Footprint
more.description: JOHANNESBURG — Nissan has redirected a planned $45 million (approximately R740 million) investment from South Africa to Egypt, underscoring a strategic pivot in its African manufacturing footprint as the company restructures globally and targets cost-efficient growth markets. The Japanese automaker, according to Business Insider Africa, will deploy the capital to expand its production capacity in [...] The post Nissan Redirects $45m Investment to Egypt, Scaling Back South African Manufacturing Footprint appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: ‘Michael’ moonwalks to $97 million opening, shattering record for music biopics
more.description: NEW YORK — “Michael,” the big-budget Michael Jackson spectacle, shrugged off bad reviews and a troubled production to launch with $97 million in U.S. and Canada theaters, according to studio estimates Sunday, shattering a record debut for music biopics. more.title: ‘Spies inside the Holy See’: Report reveals US espionage campaign targeting Pope Leo XIV
more.description: The administration of US President Donald Trump has been “spying” on Pope Leo XIV as part of a years-long intelligence campaign by Washington against the Vatican, US investigative journalist Ken Klippenstein said in a report released on 24 April. more.title: Belarus Backs Zimbabwe Grain Storage Upgrade as Agricultural, Energy Ties Deepen
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe is moving to strengthen its agricultural value chain through a major grain storage modernisation programme being implemented in partnership with Belarus, according to diplomatic sources. more.title: Dortmund seals Champions League qualification, Stuttgart’s hopes hit a bump
more.description: BERLIN — Borussia Dortmund sealed Champions League qualification on Sunday with an easy 4-0 win over a distracted Freiburg team in the Bundesliga.
